asset_name: Snow ground atlas — Settlement tile set
shared_properties: Tiles are authored for a fixed-camera village layout and maintain crisp 1px edges under nearest-neighbor scaling.
Core palette fields remain fixed at primary #111111, background #000000, and accent #FFFFFF.
The atlas supports walkable snow, packed paths, drift edges, and foundation patches inside one texture.
differences: The atlas supplies sixteen 64x64px cells for core ground surfaces and border transitions.
Packed-path cells use darker midtones so building entrances and routes stand out from fresh snow.
Drift-edge tiles add soft snow buildup along the settlement perimeter without creating false collision cues.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the winter survival loop, queue-memory mechanic, building roster, and 1920x1080 Bevy target.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_village_scene
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the settlement composition, building spacing, and whiteout layering over the frozen basin.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: TextureAtlas tile sheet loaded into world terrain systems for a single-screen village layout.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world/art_000010.png during terrain boot and create atlas indices for each ground material in the settlement map.
runtime_notes: Keep tile edges aligned to a 64px grid so path seams remain clean in the fixed overview camera.
Use repeated atlas indices rather than texture scaling for larger snow fields.
Place darker path tiles beneath buildings and queue focus points to improve navigation readability.

context.usage: Ground atlas for the settlement footprint, paths, snow drifts, and building foundations.

shape.silhouette: Sixteen square terrain cells covering bright snow, packed paths, drift borders, and dark foundation patches.

acceptance_criteria: PNG canvas is exactly 256x256px with sixteen 64x64px tile cells.
Path tiles are visibly darker than fresh snow tiles so routes remain readable in grayscale.
Tile borders line up cleanly on a 64px grid without stray anti-aliased pixels.
